item.title: Program
item.description:
Path: Notes/Bioinformatics/Introdução à Bioinformárica/Programa.md
Created: 2024-09-02 14:55
Updated: 2024-09-02 14:55
Source: Program
Subject: Program
Tags:
Ontology parent::Introdução à Bioinformática




Topic 0: Ontologies & Knowledge Representation


Lecture 1: Biological Sequences
Support Topic 1: Relational Databases
Hands-on Session 1: Protocolo_1_Registo_Armazenamento_Sequências
Lecture 2: Algorithms for Bioinformatics
Additional Topic 2: Translated Regions Detection
Hands-on Session 2: Hands-on Class - AP02 - Algorithms in Action
item.dc:creator: jmgcfdea

item.title: Translated Regions Detection
item.description:
Path: Notes/Bioinformatics/Introdução à Bioinformárica/Translated Regions Detection.md
Created: 2024-09-16 15:59
Updated: 2024-09-16 15:59
Source: Translated Regions Detection
Subject: Translated Regions Detection
Tags:
Ontology parent::Algorithms for Bioinformatics Program Coding region prediction Ab-initio gene prediction Genetic code Hidden Markov model Workflows GFF3



Where to begin?
.


Gene gene
Definition “A unit of DNA which performs one function. Usually, this is equated with the production of one RNA or one protein. A gene contains coding regions, introns, untranslated regions and control regions.”
Source: EBI::2can
.
Includes
Coding regions
Introns
Non-translated regions
Regulatory regions
Coding Sequence (CDS)
Definition “The portion of a gene or an mRNA which actually codes for a protein. Introns are not coding sequences; nor are the 5' or 3' untranslated regions (or the flanking regions, for that matter - they are not even transcribed into mRNA).”
Source: EBI::2can
.
Limitations
Sequence based
Introns excluded
Only protein-coding genes

.
Open Reading Frame (ORF)
Definition “A sequence of successive nucleotide triplets that are read as CODONS specifying AMINO ACIDS and begin with an INITIATOR CODON and end with a stop codon ( CODON, TERMINATOR).”
Source: NCBI(http://www.nlm.nih.gov/cgi/mesh/2009/ ; UniqueID: D016366)
.
Limitations
Codon-dependent reading
Similar to CDS, but usually used as reading interpretation.

Expressed Sequence Tag (EST)
Definition “A small sequence from an expressed gene that can be amplified by PCR. ESTs act as physical markers for cloning and full length sequencing of the cDNAs of expressed genes. Typically identified by purifying mRNAs, converting to cDNAs, and then sequencing a portion of the cDNAs. .”
Source: EBI::2can
.
Making of
.
Visualized
.


Phylogenetic Domains
Different challenges
Gene density
Bacteria
~1 gene / kbp
CDS represent circa 70%
M. tuberculosis
.
Eukarya
~0.01 gene / kbp
CDS represent circa 5%
[! M. musculus]
Detail

.
Chromosome geometry
Bacteria
Circular
Eukarya
Linear
ORF arrangement
Eukarya
May present Introns
The ORF may be present across the 3 available Reading Frames.
Both chains may present concurrent genes
Transcription may vary for different conditions for the same gene
Bacteria
Uninterrupted
No concurrent coding in both chains
Bacterial operon
Heterologous sequences may be present
Temperate phages
Transposons
Supplemental data availability
Homologous sets of
Protein sequences
cDNA or EST libraries



Orfing
Mapping Start and Stop Codons.
Using a suitable Genetic Code
For each available Reading Frame
3 per Chain


Stretches of sequence without a Stop codon, and beginning with a Start codon
Putative ORF
The longer the putative ORF
The more plausible its existence will be
Small ORFs
Even real
Will not be considered.
Typical success rate
< 85% of the real ORFs present
Too low to be really useful.
Limitations
No introns may be present
Example Implementation
NCBI::ORF Finder

.
Matching to Previously Trained Hidden Markov Model (HMM)
Probabilistic model that relates tangible states (observable) to hidden ones conveying a feature (e.g. being a gene)
A gentle introduction
Getting serious
In fact
Training of the Models is crucial for the result quality


Nobody relies on ab initio tools anymore

.
Workflows feeding a pipeline may enhance the quality of the prediction by chaining
Masker for putative heterologous sequences (RepeatMasker)
Parallel ab initio tools
e.g. Augustus, SNAP, etc
Evidence based gene prediction (proteins, EST, etc)
Consolidation of all the prediction through consensus
Refinement of the boundaries of the predictions (e.g. Exonerate)
Unified annotation for the sequence

.
Complexity is not a substitute for training quality



Formats
Requirement for being open-ended
Preserving the ability to grow in a organic fashion
The sequence may be absent
Case Study
GFF3
Tabular nature

Hierarchical Features
Molecule
Gene
CDS
Exon
Transcript
UTR
etc
Example
.
Intended for being visualized graphically
Generic example
.
Example Tools
SANGER::Artemis
Mainly a java based editor
GMOD::Apollo
Intended for server-side
item.dc:creator: jmgcfdea
